methods.description: Analysis of the stratigraphy and sedimentology of continental carbonates in the field and laboratory, combined with available dates, to assess the time of deposition.

steps.description: Four stratigraphic sections were measured in each studied area.
steps.searchable_summary: Measure stratigraphic sections in the field

steps.description: The field and laboratory analysis of the stratigraphy and sedimentology of the continental carbonates combined with the available dates for the main outcrops led to the assessment of the time of deposition of the various continental carbonates.
steps.searchable_summary: Combine stratigraphic, sedimentological, and geochronological data to assess deposition time

methods.description: Production of cartographic representation of continental carbonate occurrences by associating field data, existing maps, and Shuttle Radar Topography Mission (SRTM) images.

steps.description: The cartographic representation of the continental carbonate occurrences was produced by associating field data, existing maps and images from the Shuttle Radar Topography Mission (S 15 to 23; W 54 to 60; version 3 -SRTM3).
steps.searchable_summary: Gather and associate spatial data sources including field data, existing maps, and SRTM images
inputs: SRTM images (S 15 to 23; W 54 to 60; version 3 -SRTM3)

steps.description: The SRTM data were combined into a Digital Elevation Model (DEM)
steps.searchable_summary: Generate a Digital Elevation Model (DEM) from SRTM data

steps.description: then associated with the field data, using 52 o north ILWIS 3.8 free software.
steps.searchable_summary: Integrate field data with the generated DEM using GIS software

steps.description: Maps from other authors (Boggiani and Coimbra 1995, Corrêa et al. 2011, Sallun Filho and Karmann 2007) were digitalized
steps.searchable_summary: Digitalize existing maps from external sources

steps.description: and combined with the DEM images to generate an initial continental carbonate map.
steps.searchable_summary: Combine digitalized maps and DEM data to create an initial continental carbonate map

steps.description: All the maps were finished in Inkscape free software.
steps.searchable_summary: Refine and finalize maps using graphic design software

methods.description: Collection of sediment samples from different carbonate facies for subsequent laboratory analysis.

steps.description: Several tens of samples of the different carbonate facies were collected for analysis of their sedimentological features and to support field descriptions and interpretations.
steps.searchable_summary: Collect geological samples from different facies in the field
inputs: Different carbonate facies in studied areas

methods.description: Analysis of sediment samples using conventional optical microscopy to determine mineralogy, textures, components, structures, matrix type, and paleontology for genesis interpretation.

steps.description: In view of the fragility of some samples, these were previously submerged in a resin containing Epofer EX 401 and Epofer E 432 in a vacuum system.
steps.searchable_summary: Prepare fragile samples by impregnating with resin under vacuum

steps.description: A set of 58 samples was subjected to conventional optical petrographic analysis on a Zeiss AXIOPLAN 2 microscopy.
steps.searchable_summary: Perform optical petrographic analysis on prepared samples using a microscope

steps.description: Characteristics such as mineralogy, textures, siliciclastic components, structures, matrix type and paleontology were analysed to interpret their genesis.
steps.searchable_summary: Analyze petrographic characteristics and interpret sample genesis
inputs: Petrographic observations (mineralogy, textures, components, structures, matrix type, paleontology)

methods.description: Classification of sedimentary facies based on texture using a modified Dunham classification scheme.

steps.description: The classification scheme of Dunham (1962), modified by Embry and Klovan (1971) and Wright (1992), and applied by Della Porta (2015) to classify continental carbonates, based on texture, was applied to name the sedimentary facies.
steps.searchable_summary: Apply a specific textural classification scheme (modified Dunham) to name sedimentary facies
inputs: Classification Scheme: Dunham (1962) modified by Embry and Klovan (1971) and Wright (1992), applied by Della Porta (2015)

methods.method_name: Carbon and Oxygen Stable Isotope Analysis
methods.description: Analysis of carbon and oxygen stable isotopes in bulk carbonate samples using mass spectrometry.

steps.description: A set of 66 samples was selected for bulk sample analyses of carbon and oxygen stable isotopes.
steps.searchable_summary: Select samples for stable isotope analysis

steps.description: Extraction was made with a microdrill.
steps.searchable_summary: Extract sample material for analysis using a microdrill

steps.description: Isotopic determinations were carried out on a DeltaPLUS Advantage mass spectrometer at the Stable Isotope Analysis Laboratory of the Geosciences Institute of the University of São Paulo (Brazil).
steps.searchable_summary: Perform stable isotope analysis using mass spectrometry

steps.description: All the analytical processes followed the standard methods (McCrea 1950)
steps.searchable_summary: Adhere to standard analytical protocols for isotope analysis

steps.description: The results are expressed in δ‰ units and reported against the Vienna Pee Dee Belemnite (VPDB) standard (Table II).
steps.searchable_summary: Calculate and report isotope results in delta per mil (δ‰) units relative to the VPDB standard

methods.description: Dating of a specific carbonate sample using the U-series method via Inductively Coupled Plasma Mass Spectrometry (ICP-MS).

steps.description: As a result of the impure nature of the carbonate samples, with high concentrations of initial 230 Th, only one sample, a conglomerate from the Corumbá/Ladário plateau, was dated by the U-series method
steps.searchable_summary: Select a suitable sample for U-series dating based on criteria (e.g., purity, Th concentration)
outputs: Selected sample (1: conglomerate from Corumbá/Ladário plateau)

steps.description: applying a Thermo Finnigan Element I magnet-sector inductively coupled plasma mass spectrometer (ICP-MS), according to standard chemical and analytical methods (Shen et al. 2002).
steps.searchable_summary: Adhere to standard chemical and analytical protocols for U-series dating

steps.description: Date the sample at the Geochronology Laboratory using a Thermo Finnigan Element I magnet-sector inductively coupled plasma mass spectrometer (ICP-MS)
steps.searchable_summary: Perform U-series dating analysis using ICP-MS
inputs: Equipment: Thermo Finnigan Element I ICP-MS
